title: Social, Intellectual Property, and Privacy (SIPP)
subtitle: 50 questions covering civil liberties, online privacy, intellectual property rights, and security concepts.

questions.t: Censorship refers to:

Direction: Read each scenario carefully. Select TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect.

A company created a unique logo to represent its products and registered it for protection. This is an example of a trademark.

Direction: Read each scenario carefully. Select TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect.

Kevin posted his home address, phone number, and daily schedule publicly online. Oversharing personal information may increase privacy and security risks.

Jenny shared her passwords publicly on social media so her friends could access her accounts anytime. This is a safe online privacy practice.
Which government action may require a warrant in many countries?

Direction: Read each scenario carefully. Select TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect.

Citizens peacefully protesting against a government policy are exercising their civil liberty of freedom of assembly.
Which of the following can be patented?
Freedom of speech is an example of:
A peaceful rally organized by citizens is protected under:
A strong password should:
choices: Be easy to remember like your name
Be shared with trusted close friends
Be the same across all online platforms

questions.t: Which action best protects online privacy?
choices: Using public Wi-Fi without VPN security

questions.t: Which Intellectual Property right protects inventions?
Software piracy refers to:
choices: Unauthorized copying or distribution of software
questions.a: Unauthorized copying or distribution of software

questions.t: Civil liberties are important because?

Direction: Read each scenario carefully. Select TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect.

A website collects information about users’ browsing behavior through cookies to remember preferences and activity. This is related to online privacy concerns.

Direction: Read each scenario carefully. Select TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect.

A student used a short quotation from a book and properly cited the source in a classroom report. This may fall under fair use.
Intellectual Property refers to:
Copyright protection is automatically granted when:
choices: The work is created and fixed in tangible form
The author charges money for the work
questions.a: The work is created and fixed in tangible form

questions.t: Freedom of religion means:
choices: People can choose and practice their beliefs freely
The state enforces a single national religion
Religious groups are completely exempt from all laws
Religious expressions are restricted from public sight
questions.a: People can choose and practice their beliefs freely

questions.t:
Direction: Read each scenario carefully. Select TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect.

Police officers searched a citizen’s private cellphone without legal authority or permission. This may violate the person’s civil liberties and right to privacy.
Which document protects many civil liberties in democratic countries?
Which Intellectual Property right protects confidential business information?

Direction: Read each scenario carefully. Select TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect.

Marco downloaded a paid software application from an unauthorized website because he did not want to buy the license. This is considered legal software sharing.
Identity theft occurs when someone:
choices: Uses another person's personal information without permission
Creates a complex password for security
Logs out of a public library workstation
questions.a: Uses another person's personal information without permission

questions.t: Which of the following is an example of digital footprint?
choices: Information left behind through online activities
A local hard drive recovery tool
questions.a: Information left behind through online activities

questions.t: Plagiarism means:
choices: Using another person’s work without credit
Obtaining a paid stock image license
questions.a: Using another person’s work without credit

questions.t: Intellectual Property laws primarily aim to:
choices: Restrict all forms of data sharing
Limit the number of private businesses

questions.t: Which of the following may violate civil liberties?
choices: Filing an application for a patent

questions.t: Which of the following is the limitation of freedom of speech?
choices: Publishing a criticism of the government

questions.t: Which of the following is protected by copyright?
choices: A secret formula for a soft drink

questions.t: The right to due process means:
choices: Individuals are treated fairly under the law
Law enforcement bypasses standard court trials
Property can be confiscated without justification
Individuals are assumed guilty until proven innocent
questions.a: Individuals are treated fairly under the law

questions.t: Why is online privacy important?
choices: To protect personal data and security
To bypass local internet ISP firewalls
To increase views on social platforms
questions.a: To protect personal data and security

questions.t: Phishing is:
choices: A cyberattack that tricks users into giving sensitive information
An optimization protocol for network routers
A strategy for identifying syntax bugs in code
An encrypted backup system for cloud files
questions.a: A cyberattack that tricks users into giving sensitive information

questions.t: Which symbol is commonly associated with copyright?
Two-factor authentication improves security by:
choices: Utilizing two duplicate passwords for access
Enabling multiple users to share a login

questions.t: Which of the following is an example of plagiarism?
choices: Copying an article and claiming it as your own
Summarizing a paragraph with a cited source
Renting a copyrighted textbook from a library
Translating a public domain document with credit
questions.a: Copying an article and claiming it as your own

questions.t:
Direction: Read each scenario carefully. Select TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect.

A fake email pretending to be from a bank asked users to enter their account details and passwords. This is an example of phishing.
Which civil liberty allows citizens to express opinions publicly?
A trademark is used to protect:
Public Wi-Fi networks may be risky because:
choices: They automatically charge your credit card
They delete your stored browser cache
They have slow upload bandwidth limits

questions.t: Which of the following is considered personal information?
choices: The current exchange rate of currency

questions.t: Fair use allows:
choices: Limited use of copyrighted work for education or criticism
Distributing free copies of a commercial movie
Manufacturing a product using someone's active patent
Using a brand trademark to promote your own product
questions.a: Limited use of copyrighted work for education or criticism

questions.t: Online privacy refers to:
choices: Protecting personal information on the internet
Allowing public access to email folders
Preventing search engines from showing results
Refusing to connect to any online network
questions.a: Protecting personal information on the internet

questions.t:
Direction: Read each scenario carefully. Select TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect.

Carla copied an entire research article from the internet and submitted it as her own work without citing the author. This situation is an example of plagiarism.
Civil liberties are:
choices: Taxes collected to fund federal projects

questions.t: Downloading movies illegally from unauthorized websites is an example of:
Open-source software means:
choices: Software that can be modified and shared legally
Software that is strictly proprietary and locked
Software that requires an expensive paid license
Software that only executes on local offline servers
questions.a: Software that can be modified and shared legally
